Instructional reviewer must verify original explanation, prediction, misconception, retrieval, and later-use design.

alignments.mappingRationale: Candidate mapping preserves workshop-ferris-wheel as a workshop coverage and practice-depth benchmark while assigning its 29 source challenges to explicit LEARN-IT-ALL concepts; it does not copy or approve learner-facing work.
evidenceNeeded: Reviewer must inspect step-level source coverage while requiring wholly original scenario, prose, code, checks, and feedback.
Learner flow must prove guided practice, fading, retained correctness, debugging, and changed-case behavior.

alignments.mappingRationale: Candidate mapping preserves lab-moon-orbit as a lab coverage and practice-depth benchmark while assigning its 1 source challenges to explicit LEARN-IT-ALL concepts; it does not copy or approve learner-facing work.
evidenceNeeded: Reviewer must verify independent requirements differ from workshops in scenario, starter, reasoning, order, and evidence.
Representative learners must complete behavior, accessibility, changed-case, explanation, and recovery evidence without recipe copying.

evidenceNeeded: Review blueprint must retrieve mapped concepts through changed contexts, prediction, explanation, diagnosis, and implementation.
Delayed evidence must distinguish completion from retained competence and route failures into corrective practice.

Pilot evidence must measure false positives, false negatives, difficulty, discrimination, feedback, and reassessment behavior.

alignments.mappingRationale: Candidate mapping preserves exam-responsive-web-design-certification as a exam coverage and practice-depth benchmark while assigning its 1 source challenges to explicit LEARN-IT-ALL concepts; it does not copy or approve learner-facing work.

Subject, accessibility, security, and learner-pilot reviews must approve exam validity before credential use.

courseExtensions.title: Modern extension for css language and cascade
courseExtensions.rationale: Current primary sources require these concepts for modern, accessible, content-driven professional practice, but the pinned freeCodeCamp v9 blocks do not provide enough explicit evidence to credit them as benchmark coverage.
validationNeeded: Subject reviewer must confirm current authority, stability, scope, prerequisites, and benchmark non-overlap.
Instructional, assessment, accessibility, and learner reviews must validate original introduce-through-transfer work.

architectureFindings: Current blueprint maps every source block to broad legacy competency bundles; this candidate matrix instead narrows lectures to named concepts and makes workshop, lab, review, quiz, project, and exam accumulation inspectable.
Computer and browser concepts stay mapped to the benchmark but must enter just in time around the first HTML artifact rather than preserve the current thirteen-activity delay.
Concepts absent from explicit pinned v9 evidence remain separately named modern course extensions; source-block counts cannot be used to claim their coverage.

All mappings remain candidate review because source identity and topic similarity do not establish standards accuracy, pedagogical order, assessment validity, originality, accessibility, retention, or learner transfer.
gaps: Subject reviewer must inspect all 158 source blocks and their 1,553 challenge identities against the candidate concept assignments.
Every concept needs an authored I-G-F-R-A-T activity matrix plus explicit debug, correction, delayed-retention, and transfer evidence.
Workshop, lab, review, quiz, project, and exam assignments need originality and assessment-validity review before authoring scale-up.
Representative beginner and accessibility pilots must verify sequence, language, editor interaction, feedback, recovery, and independent transfer.
